# Teycan Translate — Design System

**Version:** 1.0
**Date:** 2026-05-09
**Generated by:** `/design-consultation` (gstack)
**Context source:** `~/.gstack/projects/AlexSerbinov-Telegram_bot_ai_translator/serbinov-main-design-20260509-002448.md` (office-hours doc)

> Always read this file before making any visual or UI decision. All font choices, colors, spacing, motion, and tab naming are defined here. Do not deviate without explicit user approval.

---

## Product Context

- **What this is:** Native iOS translator app. Three modes: Phrase (one-shot translation), Companion (personal interpreter in your headphones), Bridge (live mediator between two people).
- **Who it's for:** Ukrainian IT professionals living in Spain/Portugal during their first 18 months, with families. Cohort uses app for high-stakes conversations: doctor visits, school parent meetings, landlord disputes, legal/notary visits.
- **Space:** Premium consumer iOS productivity / live voice translation. Aesthetic peers: Linear, Granola, Things 3, Stripe, Krisp, Otter. Not consumer-translator (not Duolingo, not Google Translate, not iTranslate).
- **Project type:** SwiftUI native iOS app. Apple HIG compliance required.
- **Tier ambition:** Premium aesthetic from day one. Pricing path: $15/mo "Founder edition" validation tier (0–6 months) → $30–50/mo experiment after retention validated.

## Aesthetic Direction

- **Direction:** Editorial-Strict.
- **Decoration level:** Minimal. No paper grain, no decorative shadows, no warmth-by-tinting.
- **Mood:** A serious tool for serious conversations. Sharp lines, restrained palette, geometric precision. Reads "I am literate in design" without saying so. References Linear (sharp tech), Granola (typography restraint), Things 3 mature versions (calm authority), Stripe Workflows (data clarity).
- **Anti-references:** Duolingo (cartoon/gamified), Google Translate (playful consumer), iTranslate (gimmicky), Apple Translate (too soft/generic).
- **Brand storytelling:** Name "Teycan" = founder's Irish setter. Dog appears in onboarding screen 2 (origin story), About screen, and as easter-egg paw print in empty states only. Never on app icon. Never as cartoon mascot. Variant A+B locked: clean wordmark logo, dog lives in storytelling.

## Color

**Approach:** Restrained. Near-white canvas, near-black ink, single signature accent (forest green). No gradients, no decorative grays, no tinted whites.

### Light mode

| Token | Hex | Role |
|---|---|---|
| `bg.canvas` | `#FAFAFA` | App background, root surface |
| `bg.surface` | `#FFFFFF` | Cards, sheets, modals, conversation bubbles |
| `bg.surface-2` | `#F5F5F5` | Headers, table headers, hover states, inactive areas |
| `text.ink` | `#0A0A0A` | Primary text. Near-black warm |
| `text.muted` | `#525252` | Secondary text, timestamps, captions |
| `text.subtle` | `#A3A3A3` | Placeholders, disabled states |
| `accent.signature` | `#1F4A3A` | Forest green. Live indicator, mic button, primary CTA, active tab indicator, model node ring. **Used sparingly.** |
| `accent.soft` | `#E0EBE5` | Hover backgrounds, selection, accent surfaces |
| `accent.glow` | `rgba(31, 74, 58, 0.10)` | Mic button shadow, focus ring |
| `hairline` | `#E5E5E5` | Default borders, dividers, separators |
| `hairline.strong` | `#D4D4D4` | Emphasized borders (action buttons, modal frames) |
| `semantic.success` | `#2D7A4D` | "AirPods connected" status, transcribed confirmations |
| `semantic.warning` | `#C28A2D` | Cost guard 30s deadline banner |
| `semantic.error` | `#B8231F` | Stop button, error states |

### Dark mode

| Token | Hex | Role |
|---|---|---|
| `bg.canvas` | `#0A0A0A` | App background |
| `bg.surface` | `#161616` | Cards, sheets, modals |
| `bg.surface-2` | `#1F1F1F` | Headers, hover, inactive |
| `text.ink` | `#FAFAFA` | Primary text |
| `text.muted` | `#A3A3A3` | Secondary |
| `text.subtle` | `#525252` | Placeholders |
| `accent.signature` | `#3D8A6A` | Forest green lifted (more luminance for dark surface) |
| `accent.soft` | `#1A2E25` | Hover backgrounds |
| `accent.glow` | `rgba(61, 138, 106, 0.18)` | Mic shadow, focus |
| `hairline` | `#262626` | Borders |
| `hairline.strong` | `#404040` | Emphasized borders |
| `semantic.success` | `#5FA77C` | Lifted |
| `semantic.warning` | `#E0A05F` | Lifted |
| `semantic.error` | `#DC4A3F` | Lifted |

**Dark mode strategy:** Inversion plus 10–15% saturation lift on accent and semantic colors to compensate for dark surface. Surfaces are not pure black — `#0A0A0A` with `#161616` raised cards preserves depth.

## Typography

**Approach:** Sans-only. SF Pro Display Bold with tight letter-spacing for headers. SF Pro Text for body. JetBrains Mono for data, IDs, durations, eyebrow labels. No serif.

| Role | Font | Weight | Size / Tracking | Use |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Display XL | SF Pro Display | Bold (700) | 80–96pt / -0.04em | Onboarding hero, marketing |
| Display L | SF Pro Display | Bold (700) | 40–48pt / -0.03em | Section titles, About hero |
| Title | SF Pro Display | Bold (700) | 22–28pt / -0.02em | Screen titles ("Phrase", "Companion", "Bridge") |
| Headline | SF Pro Text | Semibold (600) | 17pt / -0.015em | List headers, sheet titles |
| Body | SF Pro Text | Regular (400) | 15pt / 22pt LH | Conversation text, settings descriptions |
| Body-emphasis | SF Pro Text | Medium (500) | 15pt / 22pt LH | Original transcript in bubbles |
| Caption | SF Pro Text | Regular (400) | 13pt / 18pt LH | Subtitles, secondary labels |
| Eyebrow | JetBrains Mono | Medium (500) | 11pt / 0.18em / UPPER | Section labels, mode subtitles |
| Mono | JetBrains Mono | Regular (400) | 11–13pt / tabular-nums | Durations ("2:47"), session IDs, costs |
| Mono-emphasis | JetBrains Mono | Semibold (600) | 11pt / 0.16em / UPPER | Live indicator, status badges |

**Loading strategy:**
- SF Pro: native iOS system font, no download
- JetBrains Mono: vendored as SwiftPM resource. Bundled in app, no Google Fonts CDN dependency (offline-first principle for medical/legal context where connectivity may be flaky).

**Tabular numerals:** Always enabled for `mono` role and any duration/cost display via `font-feature-settings: 'tnum'` or `.monospacedDigit()` in SwiftUI.

## Spacing

**Base unit:** 4pt (Apple convention).
**Density:** Comfortable. Not compact (Mini App had compact density that read "DIY web"). Not generous (we rejected v1 editorial-soft because it lacked rigor).

| Token | Value | Role |
|---|---|---|
| `space.2xs` | 2pt | Hairline gaps, icon-to-label |
| `space.xs` | 4pt | Inner element gaps |
| `space.sm` | 8pt | Button inner padding |
| `space.md` | 12pt | Card padding inner, list item padding |
| `space.lg` | 16pt | Bubble vertical gap, section spacing |
| `space.xl` | 20pt | Screen horizontal padding |
| `space.2xl` | 32pt | Section breaks |
| `space.3xl` | 48pt | Onboarding hero margins |

## Layout

**Approach:** Hybrid. Grid-disciplined for app screens (Phrase / Companion / Bridge tabs). Each mode has its own structure — no shared "translator app shell". Editorial-asymmetric for onboarding screens, About, Settings hero.

### Border radius

| Token | Value | Role |
|---|---|---|
| `radius.sm` | 4pt | **Default.** Buttons, lang pills, badges, tabs, pills |
| `radius.md` | 6pt | Conversation cards (Bridge turns), Companion target pill |
| `radius.lg` | 8pt | Modals, sheets, output containers (Phrase output card) |
| `radius.full` | 9999pt | Mic button (Bridge), avatars, model node ring, live dot |

**Never use:** 0pt corners (too brutalist for our tone), 12pt+ rounded corners (too soft, not strict), uniform border-radius across all elements (kills hierarchy).

### Strokes

All borders are 1pt, full-color (not opacity-based). Hairlines are visible structural elements, not decoration. Apple HIG separator style is not used — our hairlines are crisper.

### Tab bar

Apple HIG bottom tab bar with 3 tabs. Active tab has 2pt accent line **above** label (not under, not behind). Tab icons use mono geometric characters for strict feel:
- Phrase: `═` (one-shot horizontal)
- Companion: `≋` (waveform/listening)
- Bridge: `⇆` (two-way exchange)

Tab labels are SF Pro Text 10pt Semibold UPPERCASE letter-spacing 0.02em.

### Status bar / Dynamic Island

Native Apple. Not customized.

## Motion

**Approach:** Considered, sharp, measured. Not springy. Not playful. Not decorative.

| Element | Animation | Spec |
|---|---|---|
| Mic tap | Ease-out scale 0.96 → 1.0 | `.easeOut(duration: 0.15)` |
| New transcript turn (Bridge) | TranslateY(-12 → 0) + opacity 0 → 1 | `.easeOut(duration: 0.2)` |
| New transcript line (Companion) | Opacity 0 → 1 + slide up 8pt | `.easeOut(duration: 0.18)` |
| Live indicator dot | Opacity pulse 1.0 → 0.45 → 1.0 | 1.6s linear infinite |
| Tab change | Crossfade content 100ms | `.opacity` transition |
| Modal/sheet | Native iOS sheet (slide from bottom) | iOS default |
| Cost guard banner | TranslateY(-100% → 0) on appear | `.easeOut(duration: 0.25)` |
| Cost guard banner blink (last 30s) | Border-color subtle pulse | 2s ease-in-out infinite |

**Never use:** Spring physics with overshoot, breathing animations, pulse rings, decorative loaders, bounce curves.

## Three Modes — Layout Disambiguation

The user must understand which mode does what within 0.5 seconds. Each mode has its own structural metaphor.

### 1. Phrase (Voice / one-shot)

**Mental model:** Записав → переклав → озвучив. Single tap, single phrase, single output.
**Use case:** Message to landlord, supermarket complaint, quick translation of a sign.
**Direction:** From → To (user picks both).
**Audio out:** Speaker or AirPods (user choice).

**Layout structure (top to bottom):**
1. Screen header: "Phrase" (Title) + status (e.g., "Ready", "Translating...")
2. Subtitle: `JETBRAINS MONO 0.14em` — `One-shot · Tap, speak, done`
3. Lang pair selector: From pill / `⇄` swap button / To pill (4pt corners, 8pt padding)
4. Output card (8pt corner, surface bg, 16pt padding):
   - Eyebrow: `ORIGINAL · UK · 15 WORDS`
   - Original text (Body-emphasis 15pt)
   - Hairline divider
   - Translation (Body 15pt muted)
   - Action row: Speak (primary forest), Copy (secondary outline), Retry (secondary)
5. Bottom: square mic button 64pt with 4pt radius (NOT round — different from Bridge), label: `TAP TO SPEAK`

### 2. Companion (Realtime / personal interpreter)

**Mental model:** Лекція в навушниках. Continuous incoming translation. Model speaks translation directly into your ear. You don't talk back.
**Use case:** YouTube lecture, conference, podcast, friend's monologue, in-person presentation.
**Direction:** Source → Your ear (1-way).
**Audio out:** AirPods/headphones REQUIRED — no speaker fallback.

**Layout structure:**
1. Screen header: "Companion" + Live indicator (`Live · 1:23`)
2. Subtitle: `One-way · Слухаєш в навушниках`
3. AirPods status bar (16pt margin, 6pt corner, surface bg):
   - 🎧 icon (36pt square box, surface-2 bg, 4pt corner)
   - "AirPods Pro" (Headline 12pt Semibold)
   - Status: `CONNECTED · ROUTING AUDIO` (Mono-emphasis success green) OR `NOT CONNECTED · CONNECT TO START` (warning, blocking)
4. Target language picker (replaces v1 target pill — now first-class selector):
   - Card with eyebrow `→ TARGET LANGUAGE` + selectable language (e.g., `🇺🇦 UA` with chevron). Tap opens modal sheet with all 13 supported targets.
5. Live transcript stream (16pt margin):
   - Each line is a single row with left accent border (2pt forest, only on translated lines)
   - Original lines: muted italic 11pt (caption)
   - Translated lines: ink 13pt medium with forest left border
   - Live line has blinking caret `▎` at end (forest)
6. Bottom: square mic button 64pt with 4pt radius, label: `STOP LISTENING` (when active) or `START LISTENING` (when idle)

**Headphones gate:** If AirPods/headphones not connected when user taps Start, show full-screen overlay (96% opacity surface bg, blur backdrop):
- Headphones icon 40pt
- Title: "Connect headphones to start"
- Body: "Companion mode plays translation in your ear in real time. To avoid audio feedback, connect AirPods, EarPods, or any headset before starting a session."
- CTA: "Open Bluetooth Settings" → opens iOS Settings.

### 3. Bridge (Chat / mediator between two people)

**Mental model:** Розмова. Модель між двома людьми. Я кажу UA → модель → ES до співрозмовника. Він → модель → UA до мене.
**Use case:** Doctor visit, school parent-teacher meeting, landlord conversation, family meeting with foreign relatives.
**Direction:** Both ways (model auto-detects which language is being spoken).
**Audio out:** Speaker (so both people hear). User can override to AirPods if private setting.

**Layout structure:**
1. Screen header: "Bridge" + Live indicator (`Live · 2:47`)
2. Subtitle: `Two-way · UA ↔ ES · Mediator`
3. Lang pair selector (same as Phrase): A pill / `⇄` swap / B pill. NEW for v3 — Bridge needs explicit language pair so model knows which two languages to mediate (was implicit in v2, now explicit). Tap pill opens language sheet.
4. Conversation stage (flex-1, 12pt vertical padding):
   - **Vertical center axis** (1pt hairline running top-to-bottom of stage)
   - **Model node** at vertical center — 28pt circle, 1pt accent border, surface bg, 4pt outline gap to canvas, contains "M" letter (JetBrains Mono Semibold accent color). Node sits over axis.
   - Turn cards alternating sides:
     - Right turns (user / language A): right-aligned, 12pt right padding, 50pt left padding (so they don't overlap model node)
     - Left turns (other / language B): left-aligned, 12pt left padding, 50pt right padding
     - Each turn card has eyebrow `UA → ` or ` ← ES` with accent emphasis on language code, original text (Body-emphasis 12pt), dashed hairline, translation (Body 11pt muted)
5. Bottom: round mic button 64pt full circle (different from Phrase/Companion's square button — round = 2-way conversation), label: `SPEAKING · auto-detect`

**Why round mic for Bridge specifically:** the 2-way symmetric metaphor extends to button shape. Round = neutral, both directions. Square = directional / one-shot.

## Tab Naming

Final names locked:

| Code | Display | Subtitle (mode meta) |
|---|---|---|
| `phrase` | **Phrase** | `One-shot · Tap, speak, done` |
| `companion` | **Companion** | `One-way · Слухаєш в навушниках` |
| `bridge` | **Bridge** | `Two-way · Mediator` |

Tab bar order: Phrase → Companion → Bridge (left to right). Default landing tab on first launch: **Bridge** (killer feature). On subsequent launches, last-used tab.

**Onboarding rule:** Each mode requires a first-use explainer card. One sentence + one icon. Skip after first use.

## Components

### Mic Button

Two shapes by mode:
- **Round** (Bridge): 64pt diameter, full radius, accent fill, white SF Symbol `mic.fill`. Active state: live dot becomes part of button (glow ring).
- **Square** (Phrase, Companion): 64pt × 64pt, 4pt corner radius, accent fill, white SF Symbol. Strict, deliberate, "tool-like".

Both have the same shadow: `0 4px 12px accent.glow + 0 0 0 1px accent` (1pt accent stroke).

### Live Indicator

`<accent dot 6pt> + Live · MM:SS`. Dot pulses 1.6s. Time uses tabular-nums. Always top-right of screen header.

### Lang Pill (selector)

Used in Phrase (top), Bridge (top), Companion (target picker).
- 4pt corner radius
- Surface bg, hairline border
- Inner: eyebrow label (Mono 9pt UPPER 0.14em) + value (15pt Semibold)
- Tap target: full pill, opens sheet with language list
- Swap button (⇄): 28pt × 28pt, 4pt corner, hairline border, mono symbol

### Eyebrow Label

`JetBrains Mono Medium 11pt UPPERCASE letter-spacing 0.18em`, color `text.muted` or `accent.signature` for emphasis. Always preceded by `— ` (em-dash space) for editorial gravitas.

### Cost Guard Banner

Top of conversation area, only visible in last 30 seconds:
- 6pt corner radius
- Border: 1pt warning color
- Background: warning at 12% alpha
- Layout: text "0:30 до автозупинки" (left, warning text, tabular nums) + "Continue +2 хв" button (right, warning fill, white text, 4pt corner, 6pt vertical padding)
- Animation: subtle border-color pulse 2s ease-in-out

## Brand Mark

**Wordmark:** "Teycan" set in SF Pro Display Bold, letter-spacing -0.03em, ink color. Always lowercase except in `T`. Never italic. Never on a colored background — wordmark on bg.canvas only.

**No logo mascot** on app icon. Variant A+B from office-hours session 2026-05-09 confirmed:
- App icon: clean wordmark or monogram "T" only
- Onboarding screen 2: dog story + photo
- About screen: full origin story
- Empty states: easter-egg paw print (mono symbol `୧⌒୨` or custom SF Symbol equivalent at 12% opacity, accent color)

## Decisions Log

| Date | Decision | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| 2026-05-09 | Initial design system v1 (editorial-refined hybrid + warm cream + Instrument Serif) | First proposal based on office-hours doc |
| 2026-05-09 | **Direction shift to Editorial-Strict** | User feedback: "хочу строге, з різкими лініями, professional severe". Editorial-refined was too soft for medical/legal context. |
| 2026-05-09 | Drop Instrument Serif display | Strict aesthetic requires sans-only. SF Pro Display Bold tight delivers gravitas without serif warmth. |
| 2026-05-09 | Drop warm cream `#F6F1EC` for `#FAFAFA` strict near-white | Cream read "lifestyle/cooking app", not premium tool. |
| 2026-05-09 | **Accent: Forest Green `#1F4A3A`** (replaced amber `#B8541A`) | User feedback: amber read warning-orange. Forest = medical/calm/trustworthy fit, no other translator uses it. Picked from 4-color side-by-side comparison. |
| 2026-05-09 | **Tab names: Phrase / Companion / Bridge** | User direction: rename from Voice/Realtime/Chat. Phrase = one-shot, Companion = personal interpreter in headphones, Bridge = mediator between two. Brand-signature naming. |
| 2026-05-09 | Mode-specific layouts (each tab unique structure) | User requirement: "дизайн має підштовхувати юзера" — instant visual disambiguation per mode. |
| 2026-05-09 | Companion requires AirPods/headphones (full-screen blocker if not connected) | UX integrity: speaker-blasting realtime translation in public is wrong pattern (audio feedback, privacy, social friction). |
| 2026-05-09 | Bridge gets explicit lang pair selector (was implicit in v2) | Model needs to know exactly which two languages to mediate. Lang pair UI matches Phrase. |
| 2026-05-09 | Round mic for Bridge, square mic for Phrase/Companion | Round = 2-way neutral, square = directional/one-shot. Shape encodes mode. |

## Open Questions

These remain to validate during cohort calls (assignment from office-hours):
- Empty state for transcript history (likely SF Symbol + caption + paw print)
- App icon final design (wordmark "T" monogram in forest-green-on-cream vs ink-on-cream)
- AirPods Max / wired headphones detection — does Companion gate apply same way?
- Onboarding length: 3 screens or 5? (Lean toward 3 — show, don't tell)
- About screen tone — first-person founder note or third-person product story?

## What This System Is Not

- Not warm. Not Mediterranean. Not "cozy". (Earlier v1 explored this — rejected.)
- Not playful. No bouncy curves, no breathing animations, no decorative emoji as UI.
- Not maximalist. No textures, no gradients, no decorative blobs.
- Not corporate. No grey-on-grey, no stock photography, no "Built for Y" marketing patterns.
- Not enterprise. No Bloomberg-density data, no terminal-green, no monospace-for-everything.
- Not consumer-toy. No Duolingo characters, no Discord-style emoji reactions, no purple gradients.

It is an editorial professional tool with a forest-green pulse and the discipline of a medical chart.
